title: mirabilis expansa
intro: Mirabilis expansa, also known as the wild four o'clock, is a flowering plant native to North America, characterized by its vibrant red flowers that bloom in the late afternoon, hence the name. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat wounds, fever, and rheumatism, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial compounds. The health benefits of Mirabilis expansa include its potential to reduce inflammation, alleviate pain, and boost the immune system, making it a valuable natural remedy for various health conditions. Herbal preparations of Mirabilis expansa can be found in the form of teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
